_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Establish acceptable limits for the software application, network, or system.
reference_document_element_description: Define and enforce model development standards, including documentation requirements, testing protocols, approval gates, ongoing monitoring expectations, and the criteria for model retirement or replacement.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.448), DQ-T-015 (DQ, cosine: 0.439).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.3/10 (raw 0.326). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [acceptable, application, establish, limits, network, software] vs WIDAI-only terms [approval, criteria, define, development, documentation, enforce] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.889, R=0.860). P exceeds R by 0.029 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 24 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Establish acceptable limits for the software application, network, or system." and "Define and enforce model development standards, including documentation requirem" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.467 > STS 0.326 (gap +0.141). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.3%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [acceptable, application, establish, limits, network, software]. WIDAI terms: [approval, criteria, define, development, documentation, enforce].
